title: Full-stack automation product
scenario: Build a Hands-style automation product with workflow state, approval checkpoints, resumability, replay, AG-UI events, artifacts, destructive-action controls, and operator verification.
termination: Stop only when one real workflow pauses for approval, resumes, emits inspectable AG-UI events, produces an artifact, blocks or confirms destructive actions, and can be replayed from durable state.

prompts.prerequisites: Verify a typed workflow domain, explicit tool authority model, durable event store, operator UI, artifact storage path, and at least one real safe workflow target.
prompts.discovery: Read project architecture, agent runtime docs, ContentBlock/AG-UI contracts, tool permission rules, deployment constraints, and current workflow/event storage.
prompts.feynman: Explain the workflow state machine, approval boundary, replay model, and how AG-UI events represent progress, citations, thinking, artifacts, tool calls, approvals, and errors.
prompts.kbd_assess: Assess workflow personas, safe/destructive actions, resumability, replay, artifact evidence, operator controls, permissions, and failure handling.
prompts.kbd_analyze: Analyze existing agent runtime, event model, persistence layer, UI event rendering, scheduler, approval storage, and deployment profile.
prompts.kbd_spec: Specify workflow states, event types, approval gates, destructive checkpoints, artifact references, replay API, operator UI, and public workflow proof.
prompts.kbd_plan: Plan domain state first, then event persistence/projection, approval policy, UI event timeline, artifact handling, replay, runtime verification, critic, and retention.
prompts.research: Confirm current AG-UI/ContentBlock, workflow, artifact, and deployment APIs from official or local source before designing the workflow protocol.
prompts.implementation: Implement one bounded workflow slice. Make destructive actions impossible without explicit approval. Persist every decision and event needed to replay the workflow.
prompts.public_boundary_verification: Run one real workflow that pauses for approval, denies or confirms a destructive action, resumes, emits visible events, produces an artifact, and reconstructs from replay.
prompts.independent_critic: Verify the event log is complete, approvals are enforceable, destructive actions cannot bypass checkpoints, artifacts are durable, and replay matches the original run.
prompts.reflection_retention: Record workflow failures, missing event types, approval UX lessons, replay evidence, and skill/template improvements.
prompts.recovery: If replay or approval enforcement fails twice, stop and return to the state-machine spec before adding more workflows.
prompts.stop_conditions: Stop for missing authority model, unbounded tool access, unreplayable events, destructive action ambiguity, artifact loss, or simulated-only workflow proof.
